REFERENCE: Multiple critical inconsistencies were identified in this reference. (1) **DOI mismatch**: The DOI prefix '10.1126' is registered to **AAAS/Science**, not BMJ (whose prefix is '10.1136'). (2) **Journal mismatch**: The DOI contains 'NEJMc2119358', suggesting a **New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM)** article ID, yet the journal is listed as **BMJ**. NEJM's own DOI prefix is '10.1056', not '10.1126'. (3) **Article number and volume**: The volume '309' and article number 'e068037' are not consistent with standard BMJ formatting. (4) **Plausible origin**: A well-known paper with a similar title by Gire S.K. et al. (2014) was published in **Nature** (DOI: 10.1038/nature13193), not BMJ. The reference appears to be **fabricated or corrupted**, with the title, journal, volume, and DOI all being inappropriately combined from different sources.
